title: Armstrong Number

content.problemStatement:
Write a program to check if the given number NNN is Armstrong number.

**Note:** Armstrong number is a number that is equal to the sum of its own digits each raised to the power of the number of digits.


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains a single integer, NNN - The number that needs to be checked.


Output Format

Print on a single line:

`YES` if NNN is Armstrong number.
`NO` otherwise.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print YES if N is an Armstrong number, otherwise NO, each on a new line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 153 has 3 digits. 1^3 + 5^3 + 3^3 = 153, so it is an Armstrong number.
92727 has 5 digits. 9^5 + 2^5 + 7^5 + 2^5 + 7^5 = 92727, so it is an Armstrong number.
123 has 3 digits. 1^3 + 2^3 + 3^3 = 36 ≠ 123, so it is not an Armstrong number.

solution.approach: For each number we count its digits (D). Then we compute the sum of each digit raised to the power D. If the sum equals the original number it is an Armstrong number. The digit count is at most 7 (since N ≤ 10^6), so a simple loop is sufficient. The algorithm runs in O(D) per test case, which is effectively O(1).
solution.timeComplexity: O(D) per test case (D ≤ 7), overall O(T).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{private static int countDigits(int n){if(n==0)return 1;int cnt=0;while(n>0){cnt++;n/=10;}return cnt;}private static long power(int base,int exp){long res=1;for(int i=0;i<exp;i++)res*=base;return res;}private static boolean isArmstrong(int n){int d=countDigits(n);int temp=n;long sum=0;while(temp>0){int digit=temp%10;sum+=power(digit,d);temp/=10;}return sum==n;}public static void main(String[] args)throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));String line=br.readLine();if(line==null)return;int T=Integer.parseInt(line.trim());StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();for(int t=0;t<T;t++){String s;do{s=br.readLine();}while(s!=null&&s.isEmpty());int N=Integer.parseInt(s.trim());sb.append(isArmstrong(N)?"YES":"NO");if(t+1<T)sb.append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
